stories.title: Target Gigglescape Under the Sea Popping Toy Recalled — Choking Hazard

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
RECALL: Target's Gigglescape Popping Toy — 49K units recalled.

Dome detaches, exposing small balls inside. 1 child choked.

Return to any Target for a full refund. Sold Aug 2025–Jan 2026.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
If you bought the Gigglescape Under the Sea Popping Toy from Target, stop using it now.

Recalled July 2026 for choking hazard. Full refund at any Target — no receipt needed.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
Colorado Springs families — if you have the Gigglescape Under the Sea Popping Toy from Target, please stop using it.

The CPSC announced a recall on July 2, 2026, for approximately 49,000 units of this blue whale-shaped popping toy. The clear plastic dome can detach from the base, exposing small colored balls inside — a choking hazard for young children. There have been nine reports of the dome detaching, including one confirmed case of a child choking.

The toy was sold exclusively at Target stores nationwide from August 2025 through January 2026 for around $10.

What to do: Return the toy to any Target store for a full refund. You can also contact Target to request a prepaid return shipping label if you prefer to mail it back.

No receipt is required. The refund covers the full original purchase price.

If you know another family who bought this toy for their kiddos, please share this post.

*Source: CPSC.gov*

---

article.headline: Target Recalls Gigglescape Under the Sea Popping Toy: Choking Hazard, Full Refund Available

stories.title: Cheyenne Mountain Zoo Launches Little Paws Preschool — Registration Open Now

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
CMZoo is opening a state-accredited nature preschool in August — registration open now.

Little Paws Preschool | Ages 3–5 | ~15 students per class | First day: Aug 24

Email littlepawspreschool@cmzoo.org to apply.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text:
CMZoo's Little Paws Preschool opens August 24. Spots are limited — ~15 per class.

Ages 3–5 | Accredited by CO Dept. of Early Childhood | Mirrors D12 calendar

Apply: littlepawspreschool@cmzoo.org

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
Something exciting for Colorado Springs families with young children: Cheyenne Mountain Zoo is opening a brand-new nature-based preschool — and registration is open right now.

Little Paws Preschool at CMZoo will welcome its first class on Monday, August 24, 2026. The program is accredited by the Colorado Department of Early Childhood and follows the Cheyenne Mountain School District 12 calendar.

Here's the breakdown:

For 3-year-olds: Classes meet Tuesdays and Thursdays, 9 a.m. to noon, at $220.75 per month.
For 4- and 5-year-olds: Classes meet Mondays, Wednesdays, and Fridays, 9 a.m. to 1 p.m., at $460.75 per month.

Class sizes are intentionally small — about 15 students each — and teachers are licensed early childhood professionals who meet Colorado state requirements.

This is a private program, so tuition applies, but the setting is genuinely one-of-a-kind. There is no other preschool in Colorado Springs that takes place inside a zoo.

If you're interested or want more information, email littlepawspreschool@cmzoo.org to get an application.

Is this something your family would consider? Drop a comment — we'd love to hear your thoughts.

---

article.headline: CMZoo's Little Paws Preschool Opens August 24: Registration Is Open Now

stories.title: AAP Updates Iron Deficiency Screening Guidelines — First Major Revision Since 2010

x_posts.label: Post 3-1
x_posts.text:
AAP updates iron deficiency screening guidelines — first major revision since 2010.

New: universal infant screening at 9–18 months. Menstruating teens screened by age 14.

Ask your pediatrician.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

x_posts.label: Post 3-2
x_posts.text:
Iron deficiency is one of the most common — and preventable — nutrition issues in children.

The AAP's new guidelines set clearer thresholds and expand who gets screened.

Ask your child's doctor what changed.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
Something worth bringing up at your child's next well-visit: the American Academy of Pediatrics just published updated iron deficiency screening guidelines in July 2026 — and this is the first major revision since 2010.

Here's what the new guidelines say:

For infants: All babies should now be universally screened for iron deficiency between 9 and 18 months. Babies who are primarily breastfed should be screened earlier — between 9 and 12 months — because breast milk alone does not provide enough iron for older infants without supplementation.

For teenagers: All menstruating adolescents should now be screened within one year of their first period, and no later than age 14. Teens with heavy periods or low-iron diets should be screened on an ongoing basis.

The AAP also established new diagnostic thresholds: a serum ferritin of 20 ng/mL or below signals iron deficiency in children, and 30 ng/mL or below for adolescents.

If your child's doctor doesn't bring it up, it is completely reasonable to ask: "Have we screened for iron deficiency recently?" That one question could make a real difference.

Have you ever had your child tested for iron deficiency? What was that experience like?

---

article.headline: AAP Updates Iron Deficiency Guidelines for Children: What Colorado Springs Parents Need to Know

stories.title: 88th Annual Bronc Day Festival in Green Mountain Falls — Free, This Saturday

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
Free family day this Saturday near Colorado Springs:

88th annual Bronc Day — Green Mountain Falls, July 25
▸ Pancake breakfast 7:30 AM
▸ Parade 10 AM
▸ Live music, food trucks, pie contest all day

Admission: FREE

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
Looking for something free to do with your family tomorrow? Green Mountain Falls has the answer.

The 88th annual Bronc Day Festival takes place this Saturday, July 25, about 20 minutes west of Colorado Springs on U.S. Highway 24 — and admission is completely free.

This year's theme is "Let Freedom Ring," honoring both the U.S. 250th anniversary and Colorado's 150th. Retired Fire Chief Steve Murphy of the Green Mountain Falls–Chipita Park Fire Department is this year's Grand Marshal.

Here's the day's schedule:

7:30–10:00 a.m.: Pancake breakfast at Church in the Wildwood, UCC (10585 Ute Pass Ave.)
10:00 a.m.: Parade on Ute Pass Avenue
All day at Gazebo Lake Park (100 Lake St.): Live music, pie contest, art vendors, food trucks, and dancing through 3 p.m.

Bronc Day has been a Green Mountain Falls tradition for 88 years. There's something genuinely special about a community that has kept a festival going that long.

If you go, bring cash for the food trucks and wear comfortable shoes for the parade.

Are you planning to make the drive out to Green Mountain Falls this Saturday?

---

article.headline: 88th Annual Bronc Day Festival in Green Mountain Falls: Free Family Event This Saturday

stories.title: BeMySecret Cars & Anime Con Returns to Colorado Springs This Weekend

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
Colorado Springs weekend: BeMySecret Cars & Anime Con is back for its 5th anniversary.

Sat July 25 + Sun July 26 | CSE Center, 3960 Palmer Park Blvd
Kids 10 and under: FREE | Adult day pass: $30

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
This weekend in Colorado Springs, the BeMySecret Cars and Anime Con is back for its 5th anniversary — and it might be a better family outing than you'd expect.

Yes, it's an anime convention. But it's also a two-day indoor car show, which means there's actually something for everyone in the family, including the parent who doesn't know the difference between Naruto and Dragon Ball Z.

Here are the basics:

When: Saturday, July 25 (11 a.m.–7 p.m.) and Sunday, July 26 (11 a.m.–5 p.m.)
Where: Colorado Springs Event Center, 3960 Palmer Park Blvd
Kids 10 and under: FREE
Youth ages 11–17 (two-day pass): $10
Adults: $30 per day online, $40 at the gate; $50 for a two-day pass

There will be voice actors, a cosplay contest, anime and pop-culture vendors, an awards ceremony, and exclusive merchandise for the 5th anniversary.

Whether your kids are into anime, cars, or just like the energy of a convention floor, it's worth considering for a Sunday afternoon at minimum.

Is your family going? Let us know what you're looking forward to.

article.headline: BeMySecret Cars & Anime Con Returns to Colorado Springs This Weekend: Kids Under 10 Get In Free
